title: Union by rank or size

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Union by rank or size; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Union by rank or size
sources.claim: Union by rank or size attaches the shallower or smaller tree beneath the larger representative to limit height.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Union by rank or size attaches the shallower or smaller tree beneath the larger representative to limit height.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Equal-rank roots are joined and the chosen new root's rank increases once. Your goal is to implement and trace union by rank or size while preserving representatives. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Tree terminology and representation; Array representation and operations; Make, find and union. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Union by rank or size attaches the shallower or smaller tree beneath the larger representative to limit height. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Rank is a height bound rather than set size when used with compression, and ties require a consistent update. The draft therefore rejects: Union by rank attaches the larger-rank root under the smaller one.

includes: Attach smaller or shallower trees to control height.
Implement and trace union by rank or size while preserving representatives.
